headline: Mandatory Jawi Script in Vernacular Schools Sparks 148,000-Signature Petition
context: The 2019 introduction of Jawi script lessons in SJKC and SJKT Year 4 textbooks prompted a 148,000-signature petition, with implementation still contested in 2026.

cards.big: 148,000 people signed a petition against mandatory Jawi in vernacular schools.
cards.sub: The government introduced 6 pages of Jawi script in Year 4 Bahasa Malaysia textbooks.

cards.big: Jawi is the traditional script for Bahasa Malaysia.
cards.sub: Learning it has cultural value. The objection is not to Jawi itself but to curriculum imposition without consultation.

cards.big: SJKC already teaches 3 scripts: Mandarin, Malay (Rumi), and English.
cards.sub: Adding a 4th script competes for limited instructional time in an already dense curriculum.

cards.big: The implementation triggered a spike in racial rhetoric on social media.
cards.sub: Both sides used the issue as a proxy for broader identity anxieties.

cards.big: 6 pages of script became a proxy war for national identity. The reaction on all sides was disproportionate to the curriculum change.
Jawi has legitimate cultural significance. Curriculum changes have legitimate consultation requirements. Both positions are valid. The failure was process, not principle.
